title: Automated Deployment Checker
subtitle: Reducing deployment risk through automated validation
overview: A deployment preparation platform that helps development teams reduce risk before production releases by performing automated validation checks and providing clear feedback.

sections.heading: The Problem
sections.content: Development teams were frequently deploying to production without completing all necessary preparation steps, leading to failed releases, rollbacks, and wasted time.

sections.heading: The Solution
sections.content: Built an internal platform that performs automated validation checks against Jira and Bitbucket, highlights missing steps, and generates deployment plans tailored to each release.

sections.heading: Technical Approach
sections.content: The system integrates with Jira and Bitbucket APIs to pull ticket status, branch information, and PR reviews. A rule engine evaluates readiness criteria and surfaces issues before they reach production.

sections.heading: Outcome
sections.content: Reduced failed deployments significantly, improved visibility for stakeholders, and gave developers confidence in their release process.

title: ServerShards Game Hosting
subtitle: Scaling a game hosting platform from Minecraft to multi-title
overview: ServerShards started as a Minecraft hosting service and grew into a full-scale game server hosting platform supporting Rust, Factorio, Garry's Mod, and more — requiring a complete rethink of infrastructure, automation, and customer experience.

sections.heading: The Problem
sections.content: The original Minecraft-only setup was tightly coupled to a single game's requirements. As demand grew for other titles, the provisioning process was manual, slow, and error-prone. Each new game required custom configuration that didn't scale.

sections.heading: The Solution
sections.content: Redesigned the entire hosting stack to be game-agnostic. Built an automated provisioning pipeline that could spin up isolated game servers on demand, with per-title configuration templates and resource allocation handled dynamically.

sections.heading: Infrastructure
sections.content: Moved to a hybrid bare metal and VPS architecture using OVH VRacks for private networking. Terraform managed infrastructure-as-code for repeatable deployments. DNS was automated, and auto-scaling policies ensured capacity matched demand without over-provisioning.

sections.heading: Customer Experience
sections.content: Built a self-service panel where customers could deploy, restart, and manage their servers without support intervention. Integrated billing, usage monitoring, and automated backups gave users full control and confidence in the platform.

sections.heading: Outcome
sections.content: Successfully scaled to support multiple game titles with minimal operational overhead. Reduced provisioning time from hours to minutes, cut support tickets significantly, and maintained high uptime across the fleet.
